evals.prompt: Create a 7-slide investor pitch deck for a Series A startup in climate tech. Include market size, problem, solution, traction, team, and financials slides.
evals.expected_output: Generates a 7-slide HTML presentation using a pitch deck structure with AIDA copywriting formula, Chart.js visualizations for market and financial data, and investor-appropriate visual treatment.

assertions.text: Applies AIDA or a recognized pitch copywriting structure across the slides rather than generic bullet points
Generates at least one Chart.js visualization for market size (TAM/SAM/SOM) or traction data
Uses design tokens for consistent typography, color, and spacing across all 7 slides
Selects an investor-appropriate visual strategy (clean, credible, data-forward) rather than a generic template
Includes speaker notes for each slide with talking points and anticipated investor questions

evals.prompt: Build a data-heavy quarterly business review presentation. I have revenue, churn, NPS, and CAC data. The audience is the executive leadership team.
evals.expected_output: Produces an executive-formatted HTML presentation with Chart.js charts for each KPI, stat callout slides for key numbers, and minimal text optimized for a leadership audience.

assertions.text: Creates separate Chart.js visualizations for each KPI: revenue (line/bar), churn (line), NPS (gauge or bar), CAC (bar/trend)
Uses stat callout or KPI card layouts for the most critical numbers — not dense bullet point slides
Selects an executive presentation strategy: minimal text, large data, clear narrative flow
Applies consistent design tokens so charts and text slides share the same visual language
Includes a summary or highlights slide that synthesizes the most important insights at the top

evals.prompt: Create an educational presentation explaining how machine learning works to a non-technical audience. 8 slides, progressive complexity.
evals.expected_output: Generates an 8-slide educational HTML presentation using the explanation-first structure, progressive complexity, relatable analogies, and simple diagrams via Chart.js or CSS illustrations.

assertions.text: Applies an educational copywriting structure (explain → example → apply) rather than a sales or data-driven format
Increases complexity progressively slide by slide — starts with a relatable analogy, ends with practical applications
Avoids technical jargon without explanation; defines key terms visually or with callout boxes when introduced
Includes at least one visual diagram (CSS-based flowchart or Chart.js illustration) to support a key concept
Speaker notes include suggested explanations and anticipated audience questions for each slide

evals.prompt: Build a 5-slide HTML slideshow for a product launch. Use the PAS (Problem-Agitate-Solution) framework and include a call-to-action slide.
evals.expected_output: Produces a 5-slide HTML presentation explicitly structured around PAS: problem identification, pain amplification, solution reveal, proof/evidence, and CTA slide.

assertions.text: Slide 1–2 clearly articulates the target problem and agitates the pain (consequence, urgency) per PAS framework
Slide 3 reveals the solution with feature-benefit framing — not a feature list
Includes a proof or evidence slide (testimonial, stat, or case study) before the CTA
CTA slide has a single, clear action with urgency or incentive — not multiple competing CTAs
All 5 slides use design tokens and a consistent visual style — not mismatched layouts

evals.prompt: Create a presentation comparing three strategic options for expanding into the US market. The audience is the board of directors.
evals.expected_output: Generates a board-appropriate HTML presentation with a structured options comparison, decision matrix or radar chart, recommendation slide, and executive copywriting style.

assertions.text: Includes a structured options comparison using a table, matrix, or side-by-side layout — not a sequential slide per option
Uses a Chart.js radar or bar chart to visualize the options across evaluation criteria (cost, speed, risk, upside)
Applies board-appropriate copywriting: decisive, evidence-backed, concise — avoids management consulting jargon without substance
Includes a clear recommendation slide with rationale rather than leaving the decision entirely to the audience
Speaker notes include anticipated board objections and supporting data for each recommendation point
